channel.title: Typescript on Manuel Martinez
channel.description: Recent content in Typescript on Manuel Martinez

item.title: Scaling MCP Agents: Why Code Execution Beats Direct Tool Calls
item.description:
Introduction

If you’ve wired up one or two MCP servers to an AI agent, the experience feels seamless — the model discovers the tools, calls them, and returns results. But the moment you start connecting dozens of servers, each with tens or hundreds of tools, something breaks down quietly: your token bill climbs, latency creeps up, and responses get worse even though you added more capabilities.

This post unpacks why that happens, and walks through an architectural pattern — code execution — that can cut token usage by over 98% compared to naive direct tool calls. The ideas here are heavily inspired by Anthropic’s recent engineering post on this topic, but I want to present them from the perspective of someone who has built and deployed an MCP server and run into these scaling walls firsthand.
